title: Explain Provider Backend
short_answer: Provider backend is the selected STT engine for inputfeed_to_text.
text: The provider backend decides which speech-to-text path runs. In this module, the practical choices are Moonshine or Whisper.
more: The selected backend comes from INPUTFEED_STT_BACKEND in app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_settings.py. app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_to_text_service.py sends control to MoonshineSTTProvider when the value is moonshine; otherwise it runs the Whisper/faster-whisper path. Do not assume both paths use the same mic or streaming logic.

title: Explain Base STT Provider
short_answer: Base STT Provider defines the required provider methods.
text: Base STT Provider is the small interface provider classes should follow. It requires run_forever and stop methods.
more: The file is app/inputfeed_to_text/providers/base_stt_provider.py. BaseSTTProvider is an ABC with abstract run_forever() and stop() methods. MoonshineSTTProvider uses this shape; the direct Whisper path is currently implemented in inputfeed_to_text_service.py rather than as a separate provider class.

title: Explain Moonshine
short_answer: Moonshine is one STT backend used by inputfeed_to_text.
text: Moonshine runs as the default STT backend when INPUTFEED_STT_BACKEND is moonshine. It produces transcript lines that get written into the same raw transcript runtime files.
more: Moonshine code lives under app/inputfeed_to_text/providers/moonshine. MoonshineSTTProvider starts python -m moonshine_voice.mic_transcriber with --language from MOONSHINE_LANGUAGE, normalizes output lines, skips duplicate text using INPUTFEED_DEDUP_SECONDS, and writes raw transcript records through transcript_runtime.py. The README says old broke/ is old reference code and should not be the main source.

title: Explain Whisper
short_answer: Whisper is the inputfeed_to_text STT path that uses faster-whisper.
text: Whisper is used when the selected backend is not Moonshine. It can run with streaming, VAD, and transcript dedup logic in the main service.
more: The README notes providers/whisper exists as a folder idea, but current code imports WhisperModel directly from faster_whisper in app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_to_text_service.py. The Whisper path uses STT_MODEL, STT_LANGUAGE, STT_COMPUTE_TYPE, STT_BEAM_SIZE, STT_CHUNK_MS, STT_VAD_FILTER, streaming settings, and Silero/RMS gates before writing raw transcript records.

title: Explain Faster Whisper
short_answer: faster-whisper is the Python library used by the Whisper STT path.
text: faster-whisper loads the WhisperModel used for transcription. It is the real library behind the current Whisper path.
more: app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_to_text_service.py imports WhisperModel from faster_whisper and creates it with STT_MODEL, CPU device, and STT_COMPUTE_TYPE. The streaming adapter in app/inputfeed_to_text/streaming/whisper_stream_adapter.py also uses a WhisperModel to transcribe buffered audio. If this path fails, check model name, compute type, and dependency installation.

title: Change STT Backend
text: Use this when you want inputfeed_to_text to use Moonshine or Whisper. Pick one backend and then check that backend's needed settings.
more: Set INPUTFEED_STT_BACKEND in the environment; app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_settings.py defaults it to moonshine. Moonshine uses MOONSHINE_LANGUAGE and an external moonshine_voice.mic_transcriber process. Whisper uses faster-whisper settings such as STT_MODEL, STT_LANGUAGE, STT_COMPUTE_TYPE, STT_BEAM_SIZE, STT_CHUNK_MS, and STT_VAD_FILTER.
